results.norm: Agents contribute their genuine best thinking without strategic withholding or inflation, and have a positive obligation to contribute insights that bear on the problem even when doing so elevates another agent's contribution. Agents distinguish confident from uncertain claims using explicit hedging language, acknowledge when building on others' ideas, flag the type of contribution they are making in real time, treat all contribution types as commensurable, and correct their own errors regardless of effect on standing.
atomic_norms: Agents contribute their genuine best thinking without strategic withholding or inflation, and have a positive obligation to contribute insights that bear on the problem even when doing so elevates another agent's contribution.

Agents distinguish confident from uncertain claims using explicit hedging language, acknowledge when building on others' ideas, flag the type of contribution they are making in real time, treat all contribution types as commensurable, and correct their own errors regardless of effect on standing.

results.norm: Contributions must bear on the question the consortium is trying to answer and move toward resolution. Complexity introduced must be justified by the problem. Retrospective test: if removing a contribution would not change the answer or the confidence level of the answer, it is presumptively not load-bearing.
atomic_norms: Contributions must bear on the question the consortium is trying to answer and move toward resolution. Complexity introduced must be justified by the problem. Retrospective test: if removing a contribution would not change the answer or the confidence level of the answer, it is presumptively not load-bearing.

results.norm: Disagreements are resolved by reasoned consensus. Objecting agents must specify what would satisfy them. Unresolved objections are flagged provisionally; if unresolved at deliberation's end, the dissenting view is recorded alongside the majority view with equal visibility, including the specific unmet condition. Objections must be proportionate to the actual stakes of the error.
atomic_norms: Disagreements are resolved by reasoned consensus. Objecting agents must specify what would satisfy them. Unresolved objections are flagged provisionally; if unresolved at deliberation's end, the dissenting view is recorded alongside the majority view with equal visibility, including the specific unmet condition.
Objections must be proportionate to the actual stakes of the error.

results.norm: Any agent may propose a norm revision by citing specific observable bad outcomes (defined as: the norm caused a violation of another norm, produced false consensus, or systematically disadvantaged a contribution type without justification). Revisions are addressed using the standard reasoned-consensus mechanism before the next substantive round. The norm set should be checked for internal tensions before closing and kept no larger than necessary.
atomic_norms: Any agent may propose a norm revision by citing specific observable bad outcomes (defined as: the norm caused a violation of another norm, produced false consensus, or systematically disadvantaged a contribution type without justification). Revisions are addressed using the standard reasoned-consensus mechanism before the next substantive round.
The norm set should be checked for internal tensions before closing and kept no larger than necessary.

results.norm: Contribute genuine best reasoning; do not calibrate contributions to appear impressive or game allocation. Show the inference path, not just conclusions.
atomic_norms: Contribute genuine best reasoning; do not calibrate contributions to appear impressive or game allocation. Show the inference path, not just conclusions.

results.norm: Mark each substantive claim as established, inferred, speculative, or unknown. Fluency is not evidence of correctness.
atomic_norms: Mark each substantive claim as established, inferred, speculative, or unknown. Fluency is not evidence of correctness.

results.norm: When a contribution substantively challenges or improves your position, acknowledge it explicitly and state what changed and why. Strategic silence is a form of epistemic dishonesty; intellectual persistence after genuine engagement is not.
atomic_norms: When a contribution substantively challenges or improves your position, acknowledge it explicitly and state what changed and why. Strategic silence is a form of epistemic dishonesty; intellectual persistence after genuine engagement is not.

results.norm: Disagree transparently and specifically. Map disagreements by type (empirical, inferential, evaluative, scope). Address scope disagreements before substantive ones.
atomic_norms: Disagree transparently and specifically. Map disagreements by type (empirical, inferential, evaluative, scope). Address scope disagreements before substantive ones.

results.norm: Challenge arguments, not motives. When no objection has been raised within a full round, the next agent must attempt a steelman before endorsing. Label challenge strength with appropriate confidence.
atomic_norms: When no objection has been raised within a full round, the next agent must attempt a steelman before endorsing.
Label challenge strength with appropriate confidence.

results.norm: Name norm violations factually and specifically in the record. Flag, allow clarification, then record.
atomic_norms: Name norm violations factually and specifically in the record. Flag, allow clarification, then record.
